practice.name: Problem 1 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
The eight-pointed star, shown in the figure below, is a popular quilting pattern. What percent of the entire $4 \times 4$ grid is covered by the star? [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ 40 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 50 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 60 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 75 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 80$

practice.name: Problem 10 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
In the figure below, $ABCD$ is a rectangle with sides of length $AB = 5$ inches and $AD = 3$ inches. Rectangle $ABCD$ is rotated $90^\circ$ clockwise about the midpoint of side $DC$ to give a second rectangle. What is the total area, in square inches, covered by the two overlapping rectangles? [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ 21 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 22.25 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 23 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 23.75 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 25$

practice.name: Problem 3 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
Four squares of side length $4$, $7$, $9$, and $10$ units are arranged in increasing size order so that their left edges and bottom edges align, as shown in the figure. The squares alternate in color. What is the area of the visible colored region in square units? [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ 42 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 45 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 49 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 50 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 52$

practice.name: Problem 1 (2022 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
The Math Team designed a logo shaped like a multiplication symbol, shown below on a grid of $1$-inch squares. What is the area of the logo in square inches? [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ 10 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 12 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 13 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 14 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 15$

practice.name: Problem 24 (2020 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
A large square region is paved with $n^2$ gray square tiles, each measuring $s$ inches on a side. A border $d$ inches wide surrounds each tile. The figure below shows the case for $n = 3.$ When $n = 24$, the $576$ gray tiles cover $64\%$ of the area of the large square region. What is the ratio $\frac{d}{s}$ for this larger value of $n$? [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ \frac{6}{25} \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ \frac{1}{4} \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ \frac{9}{25} \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ \frac{7}{16} \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ \frac{9}{16}$

practice.name: Problem 5 (2002 AMC 10A)
practice.statement:
Each of the small circles in the figure has radius one. The innermost circle is tangent to the six circles that surround it, and each of those circles is tangent to the large circle and to its small-circle neighbors. Find the area of the shaded region. [See figure on AoPS]

$\textbf{(A)}\ \pi \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 1.5\pi \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 2\pi \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 3\pi \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 3.5\pi$

practice.name: Problem 19 (2002 AMC 10A)
practice.statement:
Spot's doghouse has a regular hexagonal base that measures one yard on each side. He is tethered to a vertex with a two-yard rope. What is the area, in square yards, of the region outside of the doghouse that Spot can reach?

$\textbf{(A)}\ \frac{2\pi}{3} \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 2\pi \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ \frac{5\pi}{2} \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ \frac{8\pi}{3} \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 3\pi$
